channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Martin Michuki, Son of Former Minister John Michuki, Dies After Long Illness
item.description: The Michuki family is in mourning following the death of Martin Michuki, son of the late former Cabinet minister John Michuki. Martin passed away on Sunday, May 3, 2026, at the age of 65 after battlin...

item.title: US military carries out strike on suspected drug-trafficking vessel in the eastern Pacific, killing three
item.description: The United States military carried out another strike on a vessel suspected of transporting drugs in the eastern Pacific Ocean, resulting in the deaths of three men. This follows a similar attack in t...

item.title: Florida‑based Crowley confirms its tanker CS Anthem safely transited the besieged Strait of Hormuz
item.description: A Florida‑based shipping company, Crowley, announced that its U.S.-flagged vessel, the chemical tanker CS Anthem, successfully navigated the Strait of Hormuz on Monday amid heightened military tension...

item.title: Trump suspends Hormuz naval operation amid progress in Iran negotiations
item.description: On May 6, 2026, the United States announced a halt to its military operation in the Strait of Hormuz as part of ongoing negotiations with Iran aimed at ending the West Asia conflict. U.S. President Do...

item.title: Melbourne Storm support Bellamy amid health news as they prepare for Wests Tigers clash
item.description: Melbourne Storm coach Craig Bellamy has recently revealed he is battling a neurodegenerative disorder, news that has deeply affected his team. Senior players, including Jahrome Hughes, received a pers...

item.title: Toronto Maple Leafs secure first overall pick in 2026 NHL Draft lottery
item.description: The Toronto Maple Leafs have won the 2026 NHL draft lottery, securing the first overall pick at a pivotal moment for the franchise. The result comes just days after the organization appointed John Cha...

item.title: Key parties make final appeals ahead of local and devolved elections in Britain
item.description: As Britain prepares for the largest set of elections since the 2024 general election, polling stations will open across Scotland, Wales, and parts of England. Voters are selecting local councillors, m...

item.title: Family appeals for help tracing Kenyan man missing in Dubai since 2022
item.description: A Kenyan family is going through deep distress after their relative, Gregory Onyango, disappeared in Dubai more than two years ago. Gregory, also known as Bobby or Greg, travelled to the United Arab E...

item.title: Efforts Continue as Navotas Landfill Fire is Half Contained
item.description: MANILA, Philippines — Authorities report that over 50% of the fire at the old Navotas sanitary landfill has been contained. Mayor John Rey Tiangco confirmed that recent drone imaging and mapping have ...

item.title: West Bengal CM maintains position after BJP wins state elections
item.description: In a surprising political development, West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee has announced that she will not resign even after her party, the Trinamool Congress (TMC), suffered a heavy defeat in ...

item.title: Federal Court grants temporary reprieve to Montreal father facing deportation
item.description: A 22-year-old Mexican man living in Montreal, known only as Miguel, has been granted a three-month stay of deportation by a Federal Court judge after it was determined that his removal could seriously...

item.title: Key Primary Contests in Indiana and Ohio and California Governor Debate Coverage
item.description: On May 5, 2026, voters in Indiana and Ohio participated in crucial primary elections that could shape the upcoming November general elections. In Indiana, several Republican state senators faced chall...

item.title: Top Nigerian News Headlines for Wednesday, 6th May 2026
item.description: Good morning, Naija! Today top news dey show say President Bola Tinubu waka go France meet global investors for Paris, dey talk about transparency, fiscal discipline, and his administration's economic...

item.title: FIFA President Justifies 2026 World Cup Ticket Pricing and Resale Policies
item.description: FIFA President Gianni Infantino has defended the high cost of tickets for the 2026 World Cup, emphasizing that the organisation must adhere to U.S. laws that permit resale at marked-up prices. The FIF...

item.title: Celebrities Celebrate Mothers on Mother’s Day
item.description: In celebration of Mother’s Day, several Filipino celebrities have shared how they honor and appreciate their mothers. The article highlights how these public figures take the opportunity to express lo...

item.title: Efforts Underway to Standardise Nikahnama and Protect Women's Rights
item.description: ISLAMABAD: The ongoing disagreements between Pakistan’s judiciary and the government regarding Nikahnama reforms have seen constructive progress thanks to the active involvement of the legal community...

item.title: Mangaung metro leadership grilled by Scopa over R1.3bn unauthorised spending
item.description: The leadership of the Mangaung Metro, led by executive mayor Gregory Nthatisi, appeared before Parliament’s Standing Committee on Public Accounts (Scopa) to account for serious financial mismanagement...

item.title: More than 30 Green Party candidates investigated over alleged antisemitic comments
item.description: More than 30 Green Party council candidates are under investigation over alleged antisemitic remarks, according to reporting by the Daily Mail, raising questions about the party’s internal vetting and...

item.title: Veronica Roth discusses her latest novel, Seek the Traitor’s Son, and the evolution of her writing
item.description: Veronica Roth, the American author best known for the hugely successful Divergent trilogy, has released a new book titled Seek the Traitor’s Son. Over Zoom from Chicago, Roth shared insights into her ...

item.title: Record-breaking early May heat drives high temperatures and electricity demand across British Columbia
item.description: Unseasonably warm weather across British Columbia has led to record-breaking temperatures and a surge in electricity demand, particularly in Vancouver and surrounding communities. Environment Canada r...
